summary: The main claim is: Emphasis on the Center: The central point of the story is the confrontation between Saint George and the dragon.
The princess and Saint George led the dragon back to the city of Silene, where it terrified the populace.

top_comment.author: noggin-scratcher
top_comment.text:
I am a human who did reasonably well academically and reads a lot (although I didn't study English in formal education past about age 16). If I'm honest, even with the breakdown, I'm not really seeing strong parallels between all pairs of sections X and X'. The first attempt that said "this is mostly just a linear progression from problem to resolution" is probably also what you'd get from my first attempt.

I would however be more than capable of emitting text saying "1 mirrors 1', and 2 parallels 2', and 3 corresponds to 3'" if I had the idea that this was what you wanted because you'd rejected my first answer and provided the sections. So long as you didn't push too hard on asking me to explain exactly how and why those particular parallels.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: If I'm honest, even with the breakdown, I'm not really seeing strong parallels between all pairs of sections X and X'.

summary: The main claim is: Instead, we need to work proactively to mitigate the risks it poses.

17: AI development reinterpreted as evolutionary process

19: gorilla argument, AIs could easily outclass humans in so many ways

20: story about an autonomous economy

21: practical AI race suggestions

----------------------------------------

23: potential AI catastrophes from accidents, Normal Accidents

25: safety culture (with nuclear weapons development examples), security mindset

26: sociotechnical systems, safety vs. capabil...

summary: The main claim is: Perhaps there are no cognitive realms, and in fact human brain, alien brain, alien!GPT and human!GPT cognition are all very similar, in some important sense.
Or would it predict alien text using similar cognitive mechanisms that current LLMs use to predict human text?
The main claim is: In the beginning, the emulation doesn't know how to implement CEV yet, and it doesn't know how humanity's CEV is specified in practice.
Suppose an emulation of Eliezer Yudkowsky, as of January 2023, discovered how to self-modify.
The main claim is: Systems like CICERO are trained through reinforcement learning with self-play.
I can think of at least three things that may concern people about CICERO: 1.

The main claim is: I think the reason that it is a very natural thought to want to incorporate neurotypical human decision-makers into alignment proposals is that we are confident (enough) that such decisions will be made carefully —or at least more carefull…

I claim, therefore, that these proposals implicitly presuppose that there are specific algorithmic properties of the human mind/brain that make us comfortable entrusting these ‘humans in the loop’ with the task of minimizing the likelihood…
The main claim is: It is worth noting that little is required from the child.
